import sys
from collections import defaultdict
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Count inversions using BIT
    # DP state: (left_end_value, right_end_value) -> min inversions
    # At each step i, we have elements a[0..i-1] in deque
    # left_end and right_end are values at the ends
    
    # sorted values for counting
    from sortedcontainers import SortedList
    
    INF = float('inf')
    
    # dp[(l, r)] = min inversions when left end is l, right end is r
    dp = {(a[0], a[0]): 0}
    sl = SortedList([a[0]])
    
    for i in range(1, n):
        x = a[i]
        # count elements < x and > x in current deque (sl has a[0..i-1])
        less = sl.bisect_left(x)
        greater = len(sl) - sl.bisect_right(x)
        
        new_dp = defaultdict(lambda: INF)
        for (l, r), inv in dp.items():
            # add to front: new inversions = elements in deque > x (they come after x)
            # Wait: x is at front, existing elements are after it
            # inversions: x > d[j] for j in existing => count of elements < x
            cost_front = less  # elements less than x that are after x in deque
            # Hmm wait, if x is at front, x is at position 1, others at 2..i+1
            # inversions (1, j): x > a[j] => count elements < x
            # Actually: adding x to front means x < existing elements that are smaller create NO inversion
            # x at front, element y after: inversion if x > y, i.e., y < x => count of elements < x
            key = (x, r)
            val = inv + less
            if val < new_dp[key]:
                new_dp[key] = val
            
            # add to back: x at end, existing elements before it
            # inversion (j, last): d[j] > x => count elements > x
            cost_back = greater
            key2 = (l, x)
            val2 = inv + greater
            if val2 < new_dp[key2]:
                new_dp[key2] = val2
        
        sl.add(x)
        dp = dict(new_dp)
    
    print(min(dp.values()))

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
